publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2025-12-20 00:04:13 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Bert Hansen papers

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Bern Hansen papers document the collecting and research activities of Bern Hansen in 20th-century gay male culture, physique photography, erotica, and related visual and commercial materials dating predominantly from the 1920s to 2000s. The papers contain amateur and professional photographs, photo-postcards, physique photography, publications, exhibition catalogues and brochures, erotica catalogues, calendars, magazines, art reproductions, narrative erotic drawings, mimeographed narrative drawings, later photographic reprints, commercial ephemera, and research files on these subjects. The materials illustrate the creation, circulation, and display of gay male imagery across underground, commercial, and gallery contexts, including early studio and snapshot photographs, physique-era publications, European erotica, and later mainstream presentations of gay art. Folder titles supplied by the donor. Additional descriptions from the donor are included, either printed in each folder or provided in digital form upon request.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Bert Hansen, Professor Emeritus of History, Baruch College of CUNY, was born in Chicago. He is a historian of science and medicine whose career spans teaching, research, and collecting in the history of medicine and 20th-century gay male culture. He received a BA in chemistry from Columbia University in 1965 and a PhD in history from Princeton University in 1974. His academic appointments included SUNY–Binghamton (1974–79), the University of Toronto (1979–84), New York University (1985–88), and Baruch College of the City University of New York (1991–2015). Hansen held research fellowships at Harvard University (1978–79) and twice at the Institute for Advanced Study (1984–85, 1997). Hansen's early scholarship focused on medieval and Renaissance science, followed by a shift during 1984–85 toward American medicine and public health of the 19th and 20th centuries, including the study of contemporary visual materials as evidence for public understandings of science and medicine. He is the author of "Picturing Medical Progress from Pasteur to Polio: A History of Mass Media Images and Popular Attitudes in America", 2009. Hansen has actively collected and donated materials related to medical history, gay activism, AIDS-era documentation, and gay male visual culture to multiple repositories, including Cornell University.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Martin Michel erotic films (Cornell University Library).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Bert Hansen papers, #7925. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: LIFE magazine, March 20, 1937, with a 4-page color spread about Paul Cadmus. LIFE magazine issue for June 26, 1964, with a 14-page article "Homosexuality in America." LIFE magazines (1970-1972) with cover stories about changing sexual mores: • Issue of August 28, 1970, "In an era of sexuality, growing concern about pornography." • Issue of April 2, 1971, "High School Pregnancy." • Issue of April 28, 1972, "The Marriage Experiments." LOOK magazine issue for January 10, 1967, with four-page article entitled "The sad 'gay' life of the homosexual."

controlaccess.subject: Gay culture
Gay erotica
Gay artists.
Gay erotic art -- United States.
Gay erotic photography.
Gay erotica -- Periodicals.
Gay men--Attitudes.

Gay men--United States
Gay pornography.

did.unittitle: 11 erotic narrative drawings for underground circulation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First two published in Out/Lines Underground Gay Graphics from before Stonewall by Thomas Waugh (Vancouver 2002), pp. 180-181.

did.unittitle: 29 American amateur photographs of gay interest

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Old black and white, random sources. Some information on the back. A mix of beach scenes, sailors, soldiers, athletes, and working men. A few are photo-postcards. Some are studio shots. 16 photographs are 3x5 inches; 7 photographs are 4x6 inches; 2 photographs are 5x7 inches; 4 photographs are 8x6 inches; or larger. The last one is a modern print of an old negative.

did.unittitle: 33 photographs and postcards of European erotica and pornography

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sculpture, wrestlers, sailors, and fellatio scenes. 1) Two soldiers in the studio, perhaps WWI. 2) Two early hand-colored photographs of young North Africans, Jeune arabe and Type arabe. 3) Two early snapshots of young men in sailor uniforms performing fellatio. 4) Ten postcard-sized photographs, some with writing in German on the back, of wrestlers. 5) Six photo postcards of Greco-Roman sculptures of nude men or gods. 6) Two photo postcards of posed "art-photographs" appearing to be ancient figures. In a studio, circa 1920s. 7) Ten early color photo-postcards of antique art.

did.unittitle: Autograph letter signed by Tom of Finland
Commercial gay ephemera/memorabilia and catalogues

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Daddy the Magazine No. 4 (1990), a 78-page booklet of ads for erotic photographs, along with personal ads, etc. Gallerie Voyeur: Antique Erotica, Vol. I (Louisville, KY, 1994). The Gay Paperback, Cat. No. 38 of Elysian Fields Booksellers, Elmhurst, NY (May 1989). HX, February 23, 1996, a weekly guide to gay events in NYC. Gay Men's Fiction Catalogue from Giovanni's Room (winter 1982-83), 36 pages. The Archive: The Journal of the LeslieLohman Gay Art Foundation, 2:2 (winter 1995), 12 pp. Plus eight single items of gay politics and gay venue advertising.

did.unittitle: Eagle's Nest poster
Erotica sales catalogues

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brochures and larger catalogues, sampling some of the erotica and pornographic art and films newly available in the 1990s. Also included are supposedly hidden-camera filmings of men's toilets and locker-rooms, politically incorrect works by Old Reliable, and a number of Tom of Finland sales brochures with a big catalogue showing how mainstream this formerly underground rebel artist had become, selling his work on coffee mugs.

did.unittitle: Gay art and erotica, 32 exhibit brochures and catalogues
Gay art and erotica, 48 examples of gay pictorial culture

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The 1980s and 1990s witnessed an explosion of gay art in museums and galleries, as illustrated by this collection of above-ground, elite presentations of gay art. The collection is not comprehensive, and it is New York-focused. Still, it shows the variety of work and venues, and it highlights the major presence of figurative and representational art in both paintings and photographs. Materials are roughly grouped as follows. (1) Fifteen reproductions of paintings and art photography (2) Thirty-two exhibit brochures and catalogues (3) Forty-eight examples of gay pictorial culture.

did.unittitle: Gay art and erotica, postcards and exhibits
Gay-Themed Postcards: Commercial and Exhibits
Later photographs of narrative art by Tom of Finland and others

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Eighteen photographs (eleven are 5 x 7 and seven are 8 x 10 or 7 x 10). Some images have the artist's signature within the drawing. (1) By Liiger, an 8-image contact sheet for ordering and 3 5x7 images. (2) Four 5 x 7 drawings of cowboy scenes from some story by Etienne, and one 5 x 7 by Stephen. (3) Two 5 x 7 photos of unsigned drawings by Tom of Finland. ( 4) One 8 x 10 of a tattoo parlor scene with three figures by Tom. ( 5) Six photos (7 x 10 or 8 x 10) of undated drawings by an unidentified artist of single figures, all youngish without showing genitals, closer in style to physique and artist's model images of the '50s and '60s.

did.unittitle: Magazines of physique photography

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: a) Eleven magazines of physique photos over a range of years. b) Rod Vol . 1, #1, probably 1960s or early 1970s; Two called Gay #1 and Gay #2 (also smaller format) have no date or publishing information, probably from the 1970s. c) Six normal 8.5 x 11 magazine size. The Young Physique for June 1960; Mr. Sun, vol.I, no. 1, June 1966. Muscle Teens, May 1967; King Sol, vol. 1, no. 2, 1968; QQ Magazine for Gay Guys Who Have No Hangups, February 1971; Inches Magazine, vol. no. 3, July 1985;

did.unittitle: Nude physique photography

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fifty-five single photographs from underground circulation in archival sleeves. Two photographs by amateurs. Twelve 4x5 inches, twenty-three 5x7 inches. Eight 8 x 10 inches. Ten pages disbound from a booklet (6 x 9 inches).

did.unittitle: Nude physique photography. Erotica sales catalogues

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A few dozen brochures and larger catalogues, sampling some of the erotica and pornographic art and films newly available in the 1990s. Also included are supposedly hidden-camera filmings of men's toilets and locker-rooms, politically incorrect works by Old Reliable, and a number of Tom of Finland sales brochures with a big catalogue showing how mainstream this formerly underground rebel artist had become, selling his work on coffee mugs.

did.unittitle: Original era photographs of narrative art by Tom of Finland

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: a. Sailor Jim story in twenty-one 8 x 10 photographs (author/artist unknown). b. Thirteen 4 x 5 photographs of scenes of seduction and sex in a softer drawing style (author/artist not indicated). c. Matador Ole! in an undated and unpaginated pirated edition as a corner-stapled set of 14 one-sided sheets, probably drawn in the 1960s. d. Navy Doc, undated and unpaginated edition as a booklet 8.5 x 11, mimeographed on soft paper. Probably drawn in the 1960s. e. Three early photos (5 x 7) of European narrative erotic drawings (dating perhaps from the 1920s. f. Two early photos (4 x 5) of drawings by Etienne. Waugh in Out/Lines, pp. 186-187. g. Eight 8xl0 photographs of Blade's story The Barn, 1948. h. Ten 8 x 10 photographs of drawings by Blade. j. Four 8 x 10 photographs of drawings by Blake. The first one in this group was published in Waugh (2002), p.177.

did.unittitle: Pen and ink collages (originals) signed by Patrick Micheal Thomas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Seven original collages by Patrick Michael Thomas (16 March 1936 to 19 April 2004). Each has the (c) symbol and a year on the front. All are 12H x 9W except Sheet G which is 11.75 x 7.25.

did.unittitle: Physique Culture. Erotica, articles and exhibits
Physique Culture. History
Physique Culture. Mail-order distribution of Erotica in the 1930s
Related sales material, flyers, calendars, and other issues

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: a) Related sales materials. Order forms for Olympia-The Summit of Physique Photography, before 1963. One includes two strips of contact prints as samples. b) Two bi-fold flyers for The Tony Sansone Gymnasium for Men in Brooklyn. c) Two books of nude photographs from Scandinavia, marked "Not for Export" (Star Models and Bizarre). d) Two issues of The Male European Nudist (MEN), no. 4 and no. 6; 1966, each 16 pages, including a few female nudes. e) Men and Art 1955 Calendar Photo Album. Color photograph on the cover; interior photos are b/w. 4.5 in. wide by 11 inches high. f) 1975 Calendar of Photography by Jim French. Large format, 8 x 8 inches. Color photographs.
